comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Plan-review advisory triage (PASS + signed). The advisories correctly flag that the dispatch-seam extraction is non-trivial and must be verified against the REAL _apply_batch body before extracting:
- T1/G6 (critical): before creating apply_handlers.py, READ applier.py:533-918 and confirm the per-action handlers are cleanly separable. If a handler closes over _apply_batch locals (the batch client, drift state, manifest accumulator, recorder), extract it as a function that takes that state EXPLICITLY as params — do NOT extract in a way that silently shares mutable closure state. Preserve the abort-without-further-Jira-calls-on-drift + idempotent-apply invariants exactly.
- G6 (major): the per-mutation step helper (drift recheck + dispatch + record) must keep the early-abort semantics (return/raise that stops further Jira calls) — verify the control flow translates faithfully when lifted out of the nested loop.
- T7/E1 (major): handle BOTH docs/architecture.md branches deterministically — if applier.py drops <800 LOC, REMOVE it from .github/module-size-allowlist.txt AND remove/My update its offenders-table row; if it stays >=800, update the row to reflect the new dispatcher/handlers split. (The structural module-size test from drag-gripe-brake will enforce allowlist==over-cap set, so the allowlist MUST match reality.)
- F1 (minor): "materially shorter" is judged by the nesting<=4 + the handler LOC moved out; not a separate measurable gate.
Approach: extract handlers with explicit params; add an import/structure test; run the full apply/idempotency/drift-abort suite unchanged. Claim + implement after romp (PR #81) merges (depends_on ff7d).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PARKED at a clean state (user paused the session here). Claimed by joe + plan-review PASSED & SIGNED + advisory triage recorded above; NO implementation started (working tree clean). Ready to resume: branch off the LATEST origin/main, then implement per the triage (extract per-action create/update/delete handlers from _apply_batch:~681-852 into a new apply_handlers.py, passing the SHARED MUTABLE state — rest_calls, deferred_creates, events_list, mutations_with_outcomes, client, repo_root, binding_store, pass_id — explicitly via a small context dataclass; preserve EXACT behavior including the soft-fail continue paths' OWN stderr RECON lines, the rest_calls counter semantics, and the HeadDrift abort). Reduce loop nesting to <=4 via a per-mutation dispatch helper. Fix the stale retry.py/LOC TODO at applier.py:4-15. Update .github/module-size-allowlist.txt + docs/architecture.md to match applier.py's NEW LOC (the drag-gripe-brake module-size structural test enforces allowlist==computed over-cap set). Run the full tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ suite (1027 tests) unchanged as the safety net.

compiled_state.description:
RISK 2 (over-cap growth). `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/applier.py` grew +117 LOC to 918 (re-heated — it was the prior 9.0× churn thrash leader). Its `_apply_batch` is a 386-LOC body at nesting depth 7. Split along the EXISTING dispatch-table seam, not by line count.

## Why
The documented remedy (`docs/architecture.md:222`) — separate the apply-dispatch table from the per-action handlers — was never executed.

## What — careful split along the dispatch seam
- Extract the per-action mutation handlers (the bodies dispatched per mutation kind inside `_apply_batch`, `:533-918`) into a NEW sibling `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_handlers.py`.
- Keep `applier.py` as the dispatcher/sequencer: HEAD-pin drift recheck loop → dispatch → record → manifest-write tail. Extract the per-mutation step (drift recheck + dispatch + record) into a helper so nesting drops to <=4.
- CRITICAL invariant to preserve: abort-without-further-Jira-calls on drift, and the idempotent apply contract.

## Scope
In: handler extraction + nesting reduction; the manifest stale-doc TODO (`applier.py:4-15` references a never-created `retry.py` and a wrong LOC figure) should be corrected/removed as part of this. Out: changing apply semantics or the subprocess boundary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `apply_handlers.py` created (>100 LOC) holding per-action handlers; `applier.py` is the dispatcher.
- [ ] `_apply_batch` nesting depth <= 4 and materially shorter.
- [ ] `applier.py` ideally drops below 800 (if so, remove from the allowlist); if not, the allowlist + architecture-doc remedy row are updated to reflect the new structure.
- [ ] The stale `retry.py`/LOC TODO at `applier.py:4-15` is corrected or removed.
- [ ] Apply/idempotency + drift-abort tests pass unchanged.

file_impact.reason: keep as dispatcher/sequencer; extract per-action handlers; reduce _apply_batch nesting to <=4; fix the stale retry.py/LOC TODO
NEW: the extracted per-action mutation handlers (>100 LOC)
if applier.py drops below 800 remove it; else update
update the applier.py remedy row to reflect the new structure
apply/idempotency/drift-abort tests pass unchanged; add an import/structure test if useful
